response.justification: Bogdan is an accomplished full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js experience and a genuine geospatial background via GISCollective and Open Green Map, but he is a poor fit for this role. The position requires modern C++ (or Rust) as a primary skill for building high-performance routing data pipelines, and his C++ exposure is limited to a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago. There is no Rust experience whatsoever. His career trajectory has moved firmly toward web technologies, cloud infrastructure, and AI tooling — not systems-level, latency-critical C++ work on navigation pipelines.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience: built and shipped the GISCollective platform, gave a FOSS4G 2019 talk, and has worked with geo-data pipelines across multiple roles
Distributed systems exposure at scale: at Sociomantic handled millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases
Strong engineering culture: consistent track record of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions across many employers
concerns: C++ and Rust are effectively absent from his current skill set — his only C++ work was a short engagement in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project; no Rust anywhere
Career direction is firmly web/cloud (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS) — no evidence of systems-level or embedded/native development in the last 10+ years
GIS experience is data-collection and mapping platform oriented, not routing-tile generation, navigation guidance, or pipeline latency optimization
